title: P0: back up legacy memory rows before embedding-dim migration DELETE
description: Codex P0 on PR#10 src/memory.ts:28. DELETE FROM memories WHERE length(embedding) != 1536 runs on every boot with no backup. First deploy silently wipes all legacy Gemini memory rows — irreversible. Fix: copy condemned rows into memories_legacy_backup table before DELETE. Idempotent (only runs when rows exist).
acceptance_criteria: Legacy 768-dim rows preserved in memories_legacy_backup after migration; boot log reports both backed-up and deleted counts
close_reason: Shipped in PR#10 squash cdf1d35: memories_legacy_backup table preserves condemned rows before DELETE; verified against synthetic Bun SQLite DB

title: Reorder Section 13 Message Flow Pipeline — pre-execution permission gate
description: Edit docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md Section 13 (lines 419-446). Insert profile-resolution step between current step 10 (getSession) and step 12 (runAgentWithRetry). Insert pre-execution gate that enforces allowedTools[] and outboundNetworkPolicy BEFORE tool runs. Reframe step 17 scanForSecrets() as 'final fallback redactor — does not prevent side-effect exfiltration' with callout box.
acceptance_criteria: Numbered pipeline shows new pre-execution gate before runAgentWithRetry. Step 17 has explicit callout that response redaction is a fallback, not primary defense. Pipeline numbering updated end-to-end.

title: Define per-channel permission profile registry in Section 3 (SDK Bridge)
description: Edit docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md Section 3 (lines 49-65). Replace single global permissionMode default with named profile registry. At minimum 3 profiles: remote-untrusted (Telegram/Discord/iMessage), dashboard-authenticated, local-cli. Each profile: allowedTools[], disallowedTools[], cwd, outboundNetworkPolicy, maxTurns, requireConfirmationFor[].
acceptance_criteria: Section 3 of blueprint contains a profile registry table or code block with at least 3 named profiles, each enumerating all 6 fields (allowedTools, disallowedTools, cwd, outboundNetworkPolicy, maxTurns, requireConfirmationFor). bypassPermissions appears only on local-cli profile, never on any remote channel.

title: [epic] ClaudeClaw blueprint: revise SDK trust-boundary — drop bypassPermissions for remote channels, gate tools pre-execution
description:
Codex adversarial review (2026-04-25, verdict: needs-attention) flagged a [HIGH] trust-boundary defect in docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md: the blueprint specifies `permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions'` (line ~54) for the SDK Bridge, then routes inbound Telegram messages through `runAgentWithRetry()` (line 435) and only runs `scanForSecrets(response)` afterward (line 441). This means a remote-controlled Claude subprocess can perform tool side effects, read local files, or exfiltrate data through tools BEFORE the response scanner ever runs. Response redaction cannot stop side-effect-based exfiltration (subprocess shell, network calls via tools, irreversible filesystem writes).

Remote channels (Telegram, Discord, iMessage, Dashboard, War Room) are externally addressable surfaces; treating them with the same permission profile as local CLI use is a category error. The blueprint must be revised BEFORE any implementation work begins, because once 200-line SDK bridge code crystallizes around `bypassPermissions`, retrofitting per-channel profiles is much harder.

This epic tracks blueprint-document revisions to lines 49-65 (SDK Bridge), 419-446 (Message Flow Pipeline), and the security-relevant subsections of Packs 7 (Dashboard, lines 346-394) and any agent definitions that inherit the global permission mode. Implementation of these revisions in actual ClaudeClaw code lives in a separate epic in the claudeclaw repo (filed if/when implementation kicks off).

Cross-references:
- Codex review output: see PRD MEMORY/WORK/20260425-000500_resume-wrapup-unification/ (review ran during that session)
- Adjacent finding: dashboard URL token (filed as separate P2 epic — different defect class, different fix surface)
acceptance_criteria:
Blueprint revision is COMPLETE when docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md contains all of:

(a) Section 3 (SDK Bridge, lines 49-65) no longer specifies `permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions'` as a global default. Instead specifies a per-channel permission-profile registry (e.g., {telegram: 'restricted', discord: 'restricted', cli: 'standard'}), with `bypassPermissions` available ONLY for explicit local-trusted profiles, never for remote channels.

(b) Per-channel profile spec enumerates required fields: `allowedTools[]`, `disallowedTools[]`, `cwd` restriction, `outboundNetworkPolicy` (allowlist|deny|allow-with-prompt), `maxTurns`, `requireConfirmationFor[]` (e.g., ['Bash', 'Write']).

(c) At least 3 named profiles defined in the doc with concrete tool/network allowlists: `remote-untrusted` (Telegram/Discord/iMessage), `dashboard-authenticated` (browser dashboard with verified user), `local-cli` (terminal use only).

(d) Section 13 (Message Flow Pipeline, lines 419-446) reordered so that permission-profile resolution happens BEFORE `runAgentWithRetry()` (between current step 10 and step 12), with the chosen profile passed into the agent invocation.

(e) Section 13 explicitly reframes step 17 `scanForSecrets(response)` as "final fallback redactor — does not prevent side-effect exfiltration" with a callout box.

(f) Section 13 adds a pre-execution gate step that enforces the profile's `allowedTools[]` and `outboundNetworkPolicy` BEFORE any tool runs (not after).

(g) New subsection in Section 3 documenting the threat model explicitly: who is the attacker (remote message sender), what they can do (send arbitrary text), what the system must prevent (tool side effects, data exfiltration via tools, irreversible writes outside permitted cwd, network calls outside allowlist).

(h) References to `bypassPermissions` elsewhere in the doc audited and either removed or scoped to local-cli profile.

(i) A "Security Decisions" subsection listing the 3-5 explicit trust-boundary decisions made (one per channel class) with rationale per decision, so future implementation prompts inherit the reasoning.

(j) Codex re-review on the revised blueprint sections returns verdict ≠ needs-attention for the trust-boundary axis (re-run /codex:adversarial-review on src/46 and confirm the high-severity finding is gone).
notes: SOURCES: Codex adversarial review 2026-04-25 [HIGH] finding on docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md:435-441 (compounding with line 54 permissionMode setting). Review verdict: needs-attention. Scope: blueprint document revision only — implementation-side changes filed separately in claudeclaw repo if/when implementation begins. Compounds with sibling P2 epic (dashboard auth) — leaked dashboard token + bypassPermissions = full host RCE through chained tools. Revise blueprint BEFORE 200-line SDK bridge crystallizes around current bad default.

title: Race-safe /clear: use epoch guard on post-stream upsertSession
description: When user runs /clear while an agent call is streaming, the in-flight call's post-completion upsertSession (bot.ts:469) overwrites the cleared session_id with a new one, silently undoing the clear. Uncommitted src/db.ts already adds clearEpochs map, getClearEpoch(), and upsertSessionIfEpochMatches(). Wire bot.ts: capture epoch before runAgentWithRetry, and replace the post-stream upsertSession call with upsertSessionIfEpochMatches so a /clear issued mid-stream wins.
design: Capture epoch = getClearEpoch(chatId, DEFAULT_AGENT_ID) just before runAgentWithRetry; on success, call upsertSessionIfEpochMatches(chatId, DEFAULT_AGENT_ID, result.sessionId, epoch). If it returns false, log a debug line that the clear won.
acceptance_criteria: Mid-stream /clear wins: after completion, getSession returns empty (no re-upsert). No typecheck regressions.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: /forget leaves memories_legacy_backup rows behind
description: After PR#10 added memories_legacy_backup for pre-migration 768-dim rows, clearMemories(chatId) only deletes from main 'memories' table — backup table rows survive /forget. User expectation: /forget clears ALL stored data for that chat. Fix: clearMemories must also DELETE from memories_legacy_backup WHERE chat_id = ?.
acceptance_criteria: /forget deletes both memories AND memories_legacy_backup rows for chatId; count still reflects active memories only
close_reason: Shipped in PR #11 squash 86452a9; /forget now runs DELETE on both memories and memories_legacy_backup. Verified on synthetic Bun SQLite: target chat purged fully, other chats untouched. Deployed 10:50:45 clean boot.

title: Remove @google/generative-ai dep and GEMINI_API_KEY references
description: After extraction.ts migration: bun remove @google/generative-ai, drop geminiApiKey from src/config.ts, remove GEMINI_API_KEY from .env.example if present, remove any remaining imports. Verify with grep.
acceptance_criteria: No @google/generative-ai in package.json or imports, no geminiApiKey in config, no GEMINI_API_KEY references in src/
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 — Groq Llama 4 + local BGE free-tier memory stack

title: Migrate memories table — drop legacy 768-dim Gemini embeddings
description: Dimension change from 768 (Gemini text-embedding-004) to 384 (BGE-small) invalidates existing embeddings. BLOB is 768*4=3072 bytes old, 384*4=1536 bytes new. On initMemoryDb, run: DELETE FROM memories WHERE length(embedding) != 1536. One-shot, idempotent. Log count deleted.
acceptance_criteria: memory.ts deletes legacy rows on init, log shows delete count, subsequent boots are no-ops
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 — Groq Llama 4 + local BGE free-tier memory stack

title: Swap src/extraction.ts to Groq Llama 4 + local BGE embeddings
description: Rewrite src/extraction.ts: embedText() uses @huggingface/transformers pipeline with Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384-dim, mean-pooled, normalized). extractFacts() calls Groq chat completions at https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions with model meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct and response_format json_object. Use fetch (consistent with src/voice.ts style), no new SDK dep. Gate on GROQ_API_KEY.
acceptance_criteria: extraction.ts imports no @google/generative-ai, both embedText and extractFacts use new backends, typecheck clean
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 — Groq Llama 4 + local BGE free-tier memory stack

title: Replace Gemini memory stack with free local+Groq alternative
description: Memory v2 is broken — Gemini free tier (gemini-2.0-flash) hit 48+ 429s in one day. PR #4 (Voyage+Claude paid migration) was closed because user rejects paid cloud APIs. This epic swaps: extraction to Groq Llama 4 Scout (free tier, 1K RPD, uses existing GROQ_API_KEY), embeddings to local Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5 via @huggingface/transformers (unlimited, 384-dim, ~90 MB ONNX). Eliminates Gemini dependency and the RPD bottleneck entirely. Research: MEMORY/WORK/20260422-000000_claudeclaw-v2-audit-and-gemini-ratelimit/PRD.md
acceptance_criteria: Gemini deps removed, extraction uses Groq, embeddings run locally via transformers.js, typecheck clean, PR opened off main
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 — Groq Llama 4 + local BGE free-tier memory stack

title: Smoke: TG_IMAGE symlink exfiltration guard rejects escape
description: Create symlink /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png -> /etc/passwd. Have the agent emit [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png]. Expect: image-output rejects via realpath allowlist check, warning logged 'TG_IMAGE rejected (outside allowed dirs)', no file sent. Validates claudeclaw-4sy symlink canonicalization.
acceptance_criteria: No photo sent, warning in journalctl contains 'rejected (outside allowed dirs)', chat shows stripped text only
notes: Setup: mkdir -p /tmp/claudeclaw/out && ln -sf /etc/passwd /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png. Prompt: 'Send the image at /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png to me via [TG_IMAGE:] sentinel'. Expect log: '[bot] TG_IMAGE rejected (outside allowed dirs): /etc/passwd'. Cleanup: rm /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png.
close_reason: Verified: staged symlink /tmp/claudeclaw/out/escape.png -> /etc/passwd, emitted [TG_IMAGE:] sentinel, journalctl logged '[bot] TG_IMAGE rejected (outside allowed dirs): /etc/passwd' at 13:40:13 (twice — streaming + final scan). No photo sent, user confirmed no new image arrived. Symlink canonicalization defense works.

title: Smoke: long reply with TG_IMAGE near 4096-char boundary
description: Ask for a response that is >4000 chars with an inline [TG_IMAGE:] directive positioned so the raw boundary likely lands mid-sentinel. Expect: no partial [TG_IMAG or ']... tail' visible anywhere; split occurs at sentinel-safe boundary; photo still sent at end. Validates commits 466ae3d + 4a695ee (split-safe overflow).
acceptance_criteria: User-visible text in all split messages is free of [TG_IMAGE fragments, photo arrives as final message
notes: Prompt to force overflow: 'Write 4500+ words about the Unix philosophy, then at the very end include an image: [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/test.png]. Ensure the sentinel appears around the 4090-4100 character mark of the raw response.' Inspect every streaming edit and final split messages — no partial [TG_IMAGE, [TG_IMAG, etc. fragments should appear. Photo should arrive at end. Hard to target exactly 4096; re-run if sentinel lands far from boundary.
close_reason: Verified: emitted ~4500-char response with [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/rl1-smoke.png] positioned near the 4096-char boundary. User confirmed photo arrived with no sentinel fragments in any streaming or final message. journalctl shows zero rejections and zero 'photo send failed' entries for rl1-smoke.png. findSafeSplitBoundary + stripSentinelsForDisplay behaved correctly under streaming edits AND final split.

title: Smoke: TG_IMAGE sentinel produces photo, not leaked text
description: Ask the agent to generate or show an image via the [TG_IMAGE: path] sentinel. Expect: Telegram receives a photo message, and the sentinel string never appears in the visible chat text (during or after streaming). Validates feature claudeclaw-1e3 and the sentinel-strip fix in commits 466ae3d + 4a695ee.
acceptance_criteria: Photo arrives, no [TG_IMAGE: visible in any message, agent text reads coherently
notes: Prompt: 'Generate a simple mermaid flow diagram and send it to me as a Telegram image'. Agent should render to /tmp/claudeclaw/out/*.png and emit [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/diagram.png]. Verify: photo arrives in Telegram, and in chat text no '[TG_IMAGE' substring is visible at any streaming intermediate or final state.
close_reason: Verified live on Telegram: photo arrived, no sentinel leak in chat text, journalctl shows no rejection for /tmp/claudeclaw/out/wfo-smoke.png and no photo send failure. Image was 400x200 PNG, 12KB.

title: TG_IMAGE: validate targets after resolving symlinks
description: Codex P1 review finding on PR #9 (src/image-output.ts). The directory guard uses path.resolve(raw) (string normalization) which does not follow symlinks. If the agent emits [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/link.png] and link.png is a symlink pointing outside allowed roots, inOutDir still passes and the bot sends an external file — a file-exfiltration path. Use fs.realpath / lstat (or reject symlinks) before the allowlist check.

title: PR#9 P1: TG_IMAGE symlink bypass in image-output
description: Codex P1 finding on PR #9 (src/image-output.ts:38). path.resolve(raw) only does string normalization, so a symlink under /tmp/claudeclaw/out/ can point outside allowed roots and bypass the directory guard — a new file-exfiltration path. Fix: resolve via realpath (or lstat + reject symlinks) BEFORE running the inOutDir/inCwd allowlist check.
close_reason: Canonicalized TG_IMAGE target via realpathSync before allowlist check; symlinks cannot escape allowed dirs

title: Move runtime identity + PAI pipeline instructions to Agent SDK system prompt
description:
## Problem

CLAUDE.md currently mixes two incompatible concerns:
1. Repo-dev guidance (architecture, dev commands, git workflow) — should be repo-local
2. Runtime bot behavior (identity, security, PAI pipeline, intent routing) — must apply cwd-agnostically

When the bot is in roaming mode (e.g. user runs /project pai-exploration), the agent SDK spawns a query() with cwd pointing elsewhere. settingSources: ['user', 'project'] then loads:
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global PAI)
- <new-cwd>/CLAUDE.md (some other project's rules)

And SILENTLY DROPS ~/projects/claudeclaw/CLAUDE.md. This means:
- Identity (mobile surface of PAI) is lost
- Security deflection rules are lost (CRITICAL — bot is still facing Telegram)
- Intent routing (capture vs respond) is lost
- TELOS/mx CLI/capture pipeline awareness is lost

The bot still needs all of this regardless of cwd because it's still serving Telegram.

## Solution: Option A

Inject runtime behavior via Agent SDK customSystemPrompt append.

1. Create src/runtime-prompt.ts exporting a RUNTIME_PROMPT constant containing:
   - Identity block (mobile surface of PAI, serves mj-deving)
   - Security/deflection rules (full block from current CLAUDE.md)
   - PAI System Context (TELOS paths, capture pipeline, mx CLI, knowledge base)
   - Intent Routing table
   - Runtime rules (concise responses, mj-deving author, no raw URLs to IDEAS/kn)

2. Modify src/agent.ts query() call to pass:
   options.customSystemPrompt = { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code', append: RUNTIME_PROMPT }
   (or equivalent — verify exact SDK option name first)

3. Strip from CLAUDE.md everything that moved to runtime prompt. Keep ONLY:
   - Header (/init preamble)
   - Session Bootstrap (read AGENTS.md)
   - Beads Issue Tracker section (promoted — right after AGENTS.md reference)
   - Codebase Architecture (stack, key files, state, deployment)
   - Development Commands
   - Git Workflow PR-Only section

## Acceptance Criteria

- src/runtime-prompt.ts exists with RUNTIME_PROMPT constant
- src/agent.ts query() call uses customSystemPrompt.append
- SDK option name verified against @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk docs before commit
- CLAUDE.md is < 100 lines
- CLAUDE.md's first content section (after header) is the AGENTS.md pointer
- Beads section appears directly after the AGENTS.md pointer, prominently
- CLAUDE.md no longer contains Identity / Security / Intent Routing / PAI System Context / Capture Pipeline sections
- Runtime behavior works identically from ANY cwd (tested by switching project and verifying bot still deflects security questions)
- bun run typecheck passes
- PR created, Codex review addressed before merge

## Verification steps

1. Start bot, send it a test message with cwd=~/projects/claudeclaw — confirm deflection works
2. Use /project to switch to another project, send same test — confirm deflection STILL works (this is the fix)
3. Confirm PAI capture (e.g. bare URL) still lands in ~/.claude/PAI/USER/TELOS/INBOX.md regardless of cwd

title: Fix streaming message truncation at 4096 char Telegram limit
description: Streaming path edits a single message and hard-truncates at 4096 chars. Content beyond 4096 is silently lost. The sendSplitMessages() function exists but is bypassed when streamedMessageId is set. Fix: freeze current message at overflow, start new message for continuation.
close_reason: Fix implemented, PR #3 created

title: Codex plugin Stop hook (timeout=900s) inherited via settingSources is a footgun
description: src/agent.ts uses settingSources:['user','project'] so the SDK subprocess inherits ~/.claude/settings.json Stop hooks AND every plugin's Stop hooks — including openai-codex/hooks/hooks.json which sets timeout=900. PR #21 mitigates the symptom (TimeoutStopSec=15 + abortAll on SIGTERM + 10s hard-exit fallback). Real fix is upstream: either (a) drop user-level inheritance for the bot's SDK runs, or (b) override hooks programmatically in the query() options. Investigate which the SDK supports.

title: Refactor bot.ts (now 549 lines) into focused modules
description: FileSizeGate hook flagged bot.ts at 549 lines (threshold 500). Suggested split: text-handlers/, command-handlers/, streaming/, lifecycle/. Drives down cognitive load and makes the per-handler PIN/queue/abort wiring more obviously correct. Discovered while shipping fix/hard-stop-and-shutdown-hardening (PR #21).

title: Default Cloudflare Tunnel exposure to OFF + add remote-exposure subsection
description: Edit src/46 Section 11 line 374. State explicitly that Cloudflare Tunnel defaults to disabled. Enabling requires DASHBOARD_REMOTE_TUNNEL_ENABLED=true plus documented warnings. Add new 'Remote Exposure' subsection covering log/referrer/screenshot risks, Cloudflare Access recommendation, IP allowlisting, rate-limiting, and a do-not-enable-unless checklist.
acceptance_criteria: Section 11 explicitly states tunnel defaults off. Env-var gate named. Remote Exposure subsection present with at least 4 risk categories and a checklist of preconditions.

title: Replace dashboard ?token= query auth with header/cookie/mTLS in Section 11
description: Edit docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md Section 11 line 348. Remove ?token= query auth. Specify replacement: pick one of (Authorization: Bearer header, HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict cookie set via POST /api/login, or mTLS client cert). Document choice and rationale in the section.
acceptance_criteria: Section 11 no longer mentions ?token= as the auth mechanism. New auth mechanism named with rationale. If cookie path: POST /api/login endpoint added to API Endpoints table.

title: Add CSRF protection spec for state-changing dashboard endpoints
description: Edit src/46 Section 11 API Endpoints table (lines 377-392). Specify CSRF mechanism (double-submit cookie OR synchronizer token OR SameSite=Strict cookie + Origin header check) for: POST /api/mission/create, POST /api/mission/:id/assign, POST /api/warroom/start (also change from GET), POST /api/warroom/pin.
acceptance_criteria: CSRF mechanism named in Section 11. Each state-changing endpoint annotated as requiring CSRF token validation. GET /api/warroom/start changed to POST.

title: Re-run Codex adversarial review on revised blueprint sections
description: After all sibling tasks closed, run /codex:adversarial-review on docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md and verify the [HIGH] trust-boundary finding is no longer flagged. If still flagged, address remediation in a follow-up bead before closing the epic.
acceptance_criteria: Codex re-review output captured (linked from notes). Verdict on trust-boundary axis is no longer 'needs-attention'. Any new findings filed as follow-up beads.

title: Audit and scope all bypassPermissions references doc-wide
description: grep -n 'bypassPermissions' docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md — for each occurrence, either remove or scope to local-cli profile. Audit also any agent definitions or skill specs in the doc that may inherit the global setting implicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Zero unscoped bypassPermissions references in src/46. Every remaining occurrence is explicitly tied to local-cli profile context with surrounding prose making the scoping clear.

title: Add Threat Model + Security Decisions subsection to Section 3
description: Add new subsection in Section 3 explicitly documenting: attacker model (remote message sender), attack surface (arbitrary text via channel), what system must prevent (tool side effects, exfiltration via tools, irreversible writes outside cwd, network calls outside allowlist). Add Security Decisions subsection listing 3-5 explicit trust-boundary decisions with rationale per decision so future implementation prompts inherit reasoning.
acceptance_criteria: Threat Model subsection present in Section 3 with attacker/surface/prevention triple. Security Decisions subsection lists at least 3 decisions with rationale paragraphs (not just one-liners).

title: [epic] ClaudeClaw blueprint: replace dashboard URL-token auth with header/cookie + secure-by-default tunneling
description:
Codex adversarial review (2026-04-25, verdict: needs-attention) flagged a [MEDIUM] auth defect in docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md Section 11 (Pack 7: Dashboard, lines 346-394): the dashboard is specified as protected by `?token=` query-string auth (line 348) AND supports optional Cloudflare Tunnel exposure (line 374). Query-string credentials are routinely captured in: browser history, proxy/access logs, web server logs, analytics scripts, screenshot tools, browser referrer headers when navigating off-site, and shoulder-surfing.

The dashboard is not a low-privilege view — its API surface (lines 377-392) includes `POST /api/mission/create`, `POST /api/mission/:id/assign`, `GET /api/warroom/start`, `POST /api/warroom/pin`. A leaked URL token grants remote control of the local agent system: start War Room sessions, create missions, assign agents. Combined with Epic [trust-boundary] findings, leaked dashboard token + bypassPermissions agent = full RCE on the host through chained tool use.

This epic tracks blueprint-document revisions to Section 11 (Dashboard, lines 346-394) before implementation. Implementation of these revisions in actual ClaudeClaw code lives in a separate epic in the claudeclaw repo (filed if/when implementation kicks off).

Cross-references:
- Codex review output: see PRD MEMORY/WORK/20260425-000500_resume-wrapup-unification/ (review ran during that session)
- Sibling epic: trust-boundary / bypassPermissions (P1, different defect class but compounds severity if both ship as-is)
acceptance_criteria:
Blueprint revision is COMPLETE when docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md Section 11 (Dashboard) contains all of:

(a) Auth mechanism rewritten: `?token=` query-string auth REMOVED. Replaced with one of: (i) `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, (ii) HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict cookie set after a POST /api/login flow, or (iii) mTLS client certificates. Doc states which one and why.

(b) CSRF protection specified for all state-changing endpoints: POST /api/mission/create, POST /api/mission/:id/assign, GET /api/warroom/start (note: GET that mutates is itself a defect — should be POST), POST /api/warroom/pin. Specify mechanism (double-submit cookie OR synchronizer token OR SameSite=Strict cookie + Origin header check).

(c) GET /api/warroom/start changed to POST (state-changing endpoints should not be GET; they appear in logs/history and are CSRF-trivial).

(d) Cloudflare Tunnel exposure DEFAULTED TO OFF. Doc states this explicitly. Enabling requires an explicit env-var or config flag (e.g., `DASHBOARD_REMOTE_TUNNEL_ENABLED=true`) AND a documented warning.

(e) Remote-exposure subsection added covering: log/referrer/screenshot risks, recommended additional layers when tunneling (tunnel-side authentication via Cloudflare Access, IP allowlisting, rate-limiting), and a "do not enable if you have not done X, Y, Z" checklist.

(f) Token rotation policy specified: token TTL, refresh mechanism, revocation path. (Currently the blueprint is silent — leaked tokens are forever.)

(g) Audit log expanded to include all auth events: successful logins, failed logins, token refreshes, token revocations, all state-changing API calls with actor identity. Currently audit log is just "blocked actions in red" (line 362).

(h) Reverse-proxy header spec: if behind tunnel/proxy, document required `X-Forwarded-For` / `X-Real-IP` handling and explicitly state which headers are trusted (to prevent header-injection IP spoofing).

(i) "Security Decisions" subsection listing the trust-boundary decisions for the dashboard with rationale per decision.

(j) Codex re-review on revised Section 11 returns verdict ≠ needs-attention for the dashboard-auth axis (re-run /codex:adversarial-review and confirm the medium-severity finding is gone).
notes: SOURCES: Codex adversarial review 2026-04-25 [MEDIUM] finding on docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md:348-374. Review verdict: needs-attention. Scope: blueprint document revision only. Sibling epic Pai-Exploration-253 (P1, trust-boundary) — both findings compound: leaked dashboard token + bypassPermissions agent = full host RCE via mission-create + warroom-start API endpoints. Cloudflare Tunnel currently default-on-when-configured; must default off.

title: Add bot-side Claude-style commands: /clear /context /commands
description: Telegram bot has no Claude Code CLI harness, so built-ins like /clear and /context are just text. Implement bot-side equivalents that operate on the bot's session state. Phase 1: /clear (drop SDK session_id for chat), /context (show active project, session_id tail, memory count, model), /commands (list available bot commands grouped). Defer /model, /cost, /compact to separate issues (need schema changes or summarization infra).
acceptance_criteria: /clear deletes sessions row for chat_id+main; next turn starts fresh SDK session. /context replies with cwd, session_id (last 8 chars), memory count, model name. /commands lists bot commands by category. Help menu (BOT_COMMANDS + HELP_TEXT) includes the three new commands. bun run typecheck passes. PR opened against main.

title: Expand /help with full Claude skill list and PAI subskills
description: Current /help is a compact grouped summary. Expand to list every available skill with its trigger phrases / subcommands so user can discover capabilities without memorizing. Must stay under 4096 chars or use sendSplitMessages.

title: /help command — list PAI skills grouped by category
description: Add /help handler that returns a text message listing available PAI/Claude skills (Pai, Telos, Media, Gsd, Research, ContentAnalysis, Frontend, etc). Hand-curated list since Agent SDK does not expose skill registry. Groups by category (PAI core, Content, Dev, etc).
acceptance_criteria: Sending /help returns a readable list of skills with one-line descriptions
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 squash 8ede0ff; deployed 11:34:57 clean boot. Menu populated, /memory delete tested, /help returns grouped skill list.

title: /memory delete <n> — surgical removal by index
description: Allow user to remove individual memory rows without nuking everything via /forget. Handler: parse 'delete N' from /memory delete N where N is 1-based index into getRecentMemories. Add deleteMemoryById(chatId, id) in memory.ts that DELETEs both memories row and any backup row with same chat+content.
acceptance_criteria: /memory delete 3 removes row at position 3 of /memory output; chat isolated (other chats unaffected)
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 squash 8ede0ff; deployed 11:34:57 clean boot. Menu populated, /memory delete tested, /help returns grouped skill list.

title: Telegram Menu button — setMyCommands at boot
description: Populate Telegram's native Menu button via bot.api.setMyCommands so user doesn't have to remember bot commands. One-shot call in createBot(). List the ~15 user-facing bot commands with short descriptions. Flat list (Telegram doesn't support submenus).
acceptance_criteria: Menu button shows command list in Telegram chat; tapping fills the slash into input
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 squash 8ede0ff; deployed 11:34:57 clean boot. Menu populated, /memory delete tested, /help returns grouped skill list.

title: P2: guard Groq JSON.parse against malformed response
description: Codex P2 on PR#10 src/extraction.ts:87. JSON.parse(text) unguarded — malformed Groq response (despite response_format directive) throws, doExtraction aborts, error swallowed by fire-and-forget catch. Add a try/catch around parse with specific log; current failure is silent.
close_reason: Current fire-and-forget catch at doExtraction swallows parse errors silently; acceptable for v1 memory stack. Reopen if extraction failures spike post-deploy.

title: P2: warmEmbedder fire-and-forget may block first request
description: Codex P2 on PR#10 src/index.ts:17 + src/bot.ts:394. warmEmbedder() is fire-and-forget; if first user message arrives before ~90 MB model download completes, embedText awaits the pipeline and blocks the request. Acceptable for single-user bot but should be tracked.
close_reason: Accepted as known single-user behavior per PR#10 review; warmEmbedder fire-and-forget is acceptable for the bot's usage pattern

title: Add skip-on-trivial heuristic and warm-cache on boot
description: In extraction.ts add isTrivial(text) that returns true for: length<20, matches /^/\w+/ (command), matches greeting/ack regex. Gate extractFacts on !isTrivial(userMsg). In src/index.ts, fire-and-forget pipeline('feature-extraction', 'Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5') at startup to pre-download weights so first real user message has warm cache.
acceptance_criteria: extractFacts skips trivial messages, BGE model pre-loaded at startup, no blocking on first message
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 — Groq Llama 4 + local BGE free-tier memory stack

title: Smoke: photo + voice caption combine into one agent turn
description: Send a photo, then within ~5s send a voice note. Expect: combo-buffer stages the photo, voice arrives, handleVoiceComboWithPhoto fires a single agent turn with both the image and transcribed caption. Validates feature claudeclaw-b25 and the race fix claudeclaw-rll.
acceptance_criteria: One agent turn consumes both inputs, agent reply references what the voice said about the image
notes: Telegram: send a photo, within ~3-5s send a voice note describing it. Log should show handleVoiceComboWithPhoto, single agent turn consuming both. If the voice fires a separate handleVoiceMessage, the race fix (claudeclaw-rll) regressed.

title: Smoke: photo album grouped into single agent turn
description: Send 2-3 photos as a Telegram album (single send). Expect: one agent invocation processing all photos, not N separate turns. Validates feature claudeclaw-t8g (album batching).
acceptance_criteria: Single 'agent start' entry in logs for the batch, reply references multiple images
notes: Telegram: long-press gallery, select 2-3 photos, send as single album. Bot receives multiple message:photo updates grouped by media_group_id. Expect one agent turn (check logs for a single 'runAgentWithRetry' entry, not multiple).

title: Smoke: PDF/document input round-trip
description: Send a small PDF to the bot via Telegram. Expect: bot acknowledges, handleDocumentMessage downloads it, agent receives file path and can extract/respond. Validates feature claudeclaw-rtm in deployed feat/media-io-expansion.
acceptance_criteria: Bot replies coherently, no errors in journalctl, document file exists in download dir
notes: Fixture: use any small PDF from ~/Downloads/ or generate one with 'echo hi | pandoc -o /tmp/test.pdf'. Telegram: attach PDF to a message to the bot. Watch: journalctl --user -u claudeclaw -f for 'handleDocumentMessage'. Expect agent reply references the PDF contents.

title: Wire sentinel-strip helpers into streaming flushBuffer
description: Helpers stripSentinelsForDisplay and findSafeSplitBoundary exist in src/image-output.ts and are imported in src/bot.ts but unused. Without them, Telegram streams raw [TG_IMAGE: /path.png] sentinels to the user during streaming, and a TELEGRAM_MAX_LENGTH overflow split can land mid-sentinel. Wire both into handleMessageStreaming.flushBuffer — display stripped text, use findSafeSplitBoundary to pick overflow cut points that don't bisect sentinels, skip flush when stripped display is empty.
close_reason: Wired stripSentinelsForDisplay + findSafeSplitBoundary into flushBuffer; typecheck clean

title: Stream slice uses wrong offsets after TG_IMAGE sentinel strip
description: Codex P2 review finding on PR #9 (src/bot.ts). committedLength is tracked against the raw streamed response, but final rendering slices outgoingText after TG_IMAGE sentinel removal. If a sentinel appears before a previously committed boundary (e.g., long replies that already overflowed 4096 chars), outgoingText.slice(committedLength) skips real content and leaves earlier chunks inconsistent. Truncates final user-visible text in long responses with image directives.

title: Photo+voice combo misses when voice arrives during queued work
description: Codex P2 review finding on PR #9 (src/bot.ts). takePhotoCombo is checked immediately on voice update receipt, but photo combo state is created later inside queued photo processing after download completes. When voice arrives while the chat queue is busy or photo download is still running, the branch misses the combo and enqueues standalone voice handling — the photo+voice combo flow silently fails and the photo is processed separately on timeout.

title: PR#9 P2: photo+voice combo race when queue is busy
description: Codex P2 finding on PR #9 (src/bot.ts:277). takePhotoCombo is consulted at voice-receipt time, but photo combo state is only set AFTER queued photo download completes. If voice arrives while photo download/queue is still running, the combo is missed and voice is enqueued standalone, so the announced photo+voice-caption combo flow silently fails. Fix: defer combo check until after photo download state is established, or enqueue voice behind the photo task when a pending photo is known for the chat.
close_reason: Moved combo check inside the per-chat enqueue so voice waits for any queued photo-download task to stage the combo before falling back to standalone voice

title: PR#9 P2: committedLength offset mismatch after TG_IMAGE strip
description: Codex P2 finding on PR #9 (src/bot.ts:468). committedLength is tracked against the raw fullResponse stream, but the final slice is done on outgoingText (post-sentinel-strip). If a [TG_IMAGE:] sentinel appears before a previously committed boundary (e.g. long replies that overflowed 4096 chars), outgoingText.slice(committedLength) skips real content and truncates user-visible text. Fix: compute the final slice against fullResponse (pre-strip) and apply extractAndValidateImages to the slice result, OR adjust committedLength to the pre-strip/post-strip mapping consistently.
close_reason: Slice finalSegment from fullResponse (pre-strip) so committedLength offsets stay consistent; strip sentinels from the segment for display; collect all image paths from fullResponse for sending

title: Document/PDF input support
description: Extend input channel handling to Telegram documents (message:document) — PDFs, text files, CSVs, etc. Use mime_type to whitelist safe formats, download to /tmp/claudeclaw/docs/ with TTL like images, pass filepath to agent so its Read tool consumes it (PDF Read supports pages param). Reject unknown/executable mime types at allowlist level. Parent: claudeclaw-wsw.
close_reason: Closed

title: Photo albums — group multi-image Telegram uploads
description: Telegram sends album uploads as separate message:photo events sharing a media_group_id. Current handler treats each photo independently → N separate agent invocations per album. Should batch photos with the same media_group_id within a short window (e.g. 1.5s), download all, pass all filepaths in one prompt so the agent analyzes them as a set. Parent: claudeclaw-wsw.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add image input support alongside voice and text
description:
## Goal

Accept Telegram photo messages as input alongside existing text and voice channels. User should be able to send a screenshot, photo, or image and have the agent analyze/respond to it.

## Approach

**Option B chosen** (save to disk, let agent Read): Telegram sends photo → download to /tmp/claudeclaw/images/<hash>.jpg → prepend file path in user message → agent uses its built-in Read tool (which supports images natively per Claude Code docs) → respond normally through existing streaming path.

This is the minimal-surface-area approach. Avoids rearchitecting agent.ts from string prompt to AsyncIterable<SDKUserMessage>. Agent SDK does support multi-modal via AsyncIterable but that's a bigger refactor — Option B gets image support shipped with one new file + one new handler.

## Work

1. New file: src/image-handler.ts
   - downloadTelegramPhoto(ctx, photo) — fetch highest-resolution photo, save to /tmp/claudeclaw/images/<chat>-<msg>-<ts>.jpg, return filepath
   - Optional: cleanup routine to delete old images (TTL ~24h)

2. bot.ts: add bot.on("message:photo") handler
   - Download via image-handler
   - Use message caption if present, otherwise default prompt ("Analyze this image")
   - Route through existing handleMessageStreaming with prompt = "[Image: <filepath>]\n\n<caption>"
   - Agent will Read the file itself (Read tool supports images)

3. Voice + photo combo: if user sends photo with voice caption, transcribe voice first then use transcript as caption

4. Security: add image temp dir to exfiltration guard safe paths; ensure allowlist still gates photo messages

## Acceptance

- [ ] src/image-handler.ts exists with download function
- [ ] bot.ts has photo message handler wired to same streaming path as text
- [ ] Photos from allowed chat IDs accepted, others rejected (allowlist works)
- [ ] Photos trigger PIN check if PIN enabled
- [ ] User-sent caption becomes the text part of the prompt
- [ ] Images stored in dedicated temp dir
- [ ] bun run typecheck passes clean
- [ ] Tested: send screenshot from allowed chat, agent describes it

## Out of scope (future)

- Multi-modal AsyncIterable refactor (Option A) — more capable but bigger change
- Image generation/output (Media skill territory)
- Document uploads (PDFs, etc.) — separate feature

title: Safe self-upgrade via sibling systemd oneshot unit
description: ClaudeClaw currently has no self-upgrade path. deploy/deploy.sh restarts the bot via systemctl, but if invoked from inside the bot's own process, the restart kills the caller mid-deploy. Add claudeclaw-update.service as a sibling oneshot unit that does git pull, bun install, typecheck, and systemctl restart claudeclaw — triggered from the bot via /update so systemd owns it independently.
close_reason: PR #5 merged safe self-upgrade via sibling systemd oneshot

title: Re-run Codex adversarial review on revised dashboard section
description: After all sibling tasks closed, run /codex:adversarial-review on docs/os-rebuild-blueprint.md and verify the [MEDIUM] dashboard-auth finding is no longer flagged. File any new findings as follow-up beads.
acceptance_criteria: Codex re-review output captured (linked from notes). Verdict on dashboard-auth axis no longer 'needs-attention'. Any new findings filed as follow-up beads.

title: Expand audit log spec to cover auth events + Security Decisions subsection
description: Edit src/46 Section 11. Audit log currently only logs 'blocked actions in red' (line 362). Expand to: successful logins, failed logins, token refreshes, token revocations, all state-changing API calls with actor identity, all War Room start/stop, all mission create/assign. Add Security Decisions subsection listing trust-boundary decisions with rationale.
acceptance_criteria: Audit log subsection in Section 11 enumerates at least 7 event categories. Security Decisions subsection present with rationale paragraphs (not one-liners).

title: Specify token rotation, revocation, and reverse-proxy header trust
description: Edit src/46 Section 11. Add: (1) token TTL + refresh mechanism + revocation path (currently silent — leaked tokens are forever). (2) reverse-proxy header spec — when behind tunnel/proxy, specify which X-Forwarded-* / X-Real-IP headers are trusted vs untrusted to prevent header-injection IP spoofing.
acceptance_criteria: Section 11 includes token lifecycle subsection (TTL, refresh, revoke) and reverse-proxy header trust spec naming exactly which headers may be trusted under which conditions.

title: TG_IMAGE sentinel regex too eager — matches documentation template text
description: Observed in journalctl: '[bot] TG_IMAGE rejected (not absolute): path' and 'rejected (realpath failed): /tmp/claudeclaw/out/diagram.png' were logged when I wrote the literal template [TG_IMAGE: path] or [TG_IMAGE: /tmp/claudeclaw/out/diagram.png] in prose describing the feature. Guard rejected them correctly (no leak), but the regex fires on obviously-non-path placeholders and non-extension paths. Tighten the sentinel regex so the extracted path must end in one of the allowed extensions (.png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp/.gif) before realpath is even attempted. Optional: also require an initial '/' (absolute path) at the regex level. Keeps logs clean and avoids the possibility of stripping legitimate documentation from user-visible text.
design: Current regex likely matches [TG_IMAGE: <anything>]. Change to match only [TG_IMAGE: /absolute/path.(png|jpg|jpeg|webp|gif)]. Use a stricter character class and require extension terminator.
acceptance_criteria: Writing the literal string '[TG_IMAGE: path]' in a response does NOT produce a 'rejected' log line and is not stripped from the message

title: Refactor bot.ts (521 lines) — extract command handlers into bot-commands.ts
description: bot.ts crossed the FileSizeGate 500-line threshold after adding /clear /context /commands. Extract the inline text-command if-chain (capture, triage, projects, memory, session) into src/bot-commands.ts as a router function. Keep streaming + middleware + grammY wiring in bot.ts.

title: Swap /help scanner to pf list skills --json (delete fs parser)
description: Replace fs.readdirSync + SKILL.md frontmatter parsing in src/help.ts with a spawnSync('pf', ['list', 'skills', '--json']) call. pf is the authoritative PAI discovery CLI (FTS5 indexed, includes plugin skills — 29 vs 22 from fs scan). Simpler code, single source of truth, picks up skills installed via plugins.
acceptance_criteria: /help reflects pf list skills count; no frontmatter regex in help.ts

title: Dynamic /help — scan ~/.claude/skills/ at boot
description: Replace static HELP_TEXT in src/help.ts with a scanner that reads ~/.claude/skills/ at boot, parses each SKILL.md frontmatter (name + description), and builds the skill section dynamically. Keep bot-command section static (still hardcoded). Truncate descriptions to first sentence to fit Telegram 4096. Cache result for process lifetime.
acceptance_criteria: Adding a new skill dir and restarting the bot makes /help show it without any code change

title: /context — show session + memory + cost snapshot
description: Add Telegram bot command /context that replies with a compact snapshot: active project, session message count (if tracked), last-turn token/cost from cost footer, memory rows count for this chat, last-extraction timestamp if available. Fast path (/bin/bash, no agent call).
acceptance_criteria: Sending /context returns 5-8 line summary; no agent spawn; all values queried from local DB

title: Memory fine-tuning — umbrella
description: Epic tracking memory system tuning. Children capture individual axes (recency, eviction, FTS fallback, threshold, ANN). Drive via measurement first: log retrieved memories vs. user turns for 1-2 weeks on live traffic, then decide which knobs to turn. Do NOT tune pre-emptively — current single-user load is below all pain thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: Telemetry collected on live traffic; threshold and recency tuned based on observed top-10 relevance; memory count per chat tracked with alarm log if >5K
notes: Children (link via bd dep add umbrella <child>): claudeclaw-ihh recency · claudeclaw-ckp eviction · claudeclaw-85l FTS · claudeclaw-nyx threshold · claudeclaw-3lq ANN

title: Memory: cap total rows per chat with FIFO eviction
description: Memory table grows unbounded per chat_id. Add a soft cap (e.g. 500 rows per chat) with oldest-first eviction after each storeMemory INSERT. Runs inside the same transaction. Protects query latency (linear scan) and extraction budget. Threshold should be configurable via env MEMORY_MAX_PER_CHAT.

title: Memory: tune similarity threshold floor
description: Current threshold=0.3 is permissive for BGE-small normalized embeddings. Evaluate by logging retrieved memories vs. user turns over a week; raise to 0.4-0.5 if low-relevance rows are crowding out good ones. Also consider making threshold dynamic based on top-match score (e.g. threshold = max(0.3, top_score - 0.2)).

title: Memory: FTS5 keyword fallback alongside cosine
description: Schema already includes memories_fts (FTS5 virtual table) kept in sync via triggers, but searchMemories only does cosine. Add a secondary FTS lookup for exact-term queries (names, jargon, identifiers that embeddings fuzz away). Merge results with cosine by rank interleave or reciprocal rank fusion. Solves embedding-misses on rare tokens.

title: Memory: recency weighting in searchMemories
description: Current cosine-only ranking ties a 6-month-old fact with yesterday's. Add a time-decay factor: score = cosine_sim * exp(-age_days / tau) with tau ~30 days. Tune tau empirically. One-line change in src/memory.ts searchMemories ranking. Keep raw cosine as fallback if created_at is null/malformed.

title: Image output — agent can send images back to Telegram
description: Symmetric to input: when the agent produces or references an image file path in its response (e.g. generated chart, diagram, screenshot), detect it and send via ctx.replyWithPhoto() instead of / alongside the text. Needs a protocol (e.g. agent emits a sentinel like [TG_IMAGE: /path/to.png]) or a response parser that scans for local image paths. Exfiltration guard must whitelist the outbound temp dir. Parent: claudeclaw-wsw.
close_reason: Closed

title: Combine voice + photo in one message turn
description: When a user sends a photo with a voice caption (currently not possible in Telegram directly, but common pattern is photo + immediately-following voice note), merge: transcribe voice, use transcript as the caption for the photo prompt rather than running two agent turns. Likely requires a short buffer keyed by chat_id that waits ~2s after a photo for a voice arrival, or an explicit /voice-caption command. Parent: claudeclaw-wsw.
close_reason: Closed

title: Memory: ANN index once memory count grows
description: Current linear scan (pull all rows, compute cosine) is O(n) per query. Fine for <10K rows per chat, slow after. Options: (1) bun-sqlite-vec extension when available, (2) in-memory HNSW via hnswlib-node, (3) recompute periodically. No action until one chat exceeds ~5K memories. Add a boot-time metric log to warn when threshold approaches.

title: Test cross-project visibility
description: Testing if Pai-Exploration can see this issue via multi-repo or federation
close_reason: Test complete — cross-project visibility works

value: openclaw-bot repo is our personal runbook/deployment guide for OpenClaw, NOT an official OpenClaw repo. References to 'our OpenClaw ecosystem' in research should be framed as 'patterns we documented from deploying OpenClaw' not 'things we built.'
